revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:市杵嶋神社 (杉並区)]][[zh:市杵嶋神社 (杉並區)]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| name = Ichikishima Shrine
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image = File:Ichikishima Shrine - Zenpukuji Park - Suginami, Tokyo - DSC01791.JPG
| location = [[Zenpukuji (Suginami)|Zenpukuji 3-chome]], {{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|qid=Q232631|lt=Suginami|1=Suginami}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, Japan, within [[Zenpukuji Park]]
| ISO = JP-13
| Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 42 | Latitude seconds = 59
| Longitude degrees = 139 | Longitude minutes = 35 | Longitude seconds = 17
| Map = Japan Wards of Tokyo
| Main deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
| Shrine Rank = 
| consecration_year = {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|qid=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū|1=Kenkyū}} 8 (1197), {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}
| Main building = 
| Other names = {{Ublist|Osoroi Ichikishima Shrine|Zenpukuji Benzaiten}}
| festival = April 8
| Religious rituals = 
| location = {{ill|Zenbukuji|qid=Q11418967|1=Zenbukuji}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|35.71638889|N|139.58805556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|qid=Q11059454|1=Ichikishima-hime}}
}}
{{Portal box|Shinto|Geography of Japan|History}}

'''Ichikishima Shrine''' (市杵嶋神社, Ichikishima Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Zenpukuji (Suginami)|Zenpukuji]], {{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|qid=Q232631|lt=Suginami|1=Suginami}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, within [[Zenpukuji Park]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Igusa Hachimangu|ja|井草八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igusa Hachimangu|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Kadokawa Japan Place name Dictionary Editorial Committee |title={{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place name Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place name Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4093011}} |volume=Volume 13 (Tokyo) |publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]] |year=1978-10 |id={{NDL|79000112}}, {{NCID|BN00734098}} |page=887}}</ref>

== History ==

The shrine's origins date back to 1189 (5th year of {{ill|Bunji|en|Bunji|qid=Q4997712|lt=Bunji|1=Bunji}}) when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} stayed in the area during the {{ill|Battle_of_Ōshū|en|Battle_of_Ōshū|qid=Q11445749|lt=Battle of Ōshū|1=Battle of Ōshū}}. Seeking water, he used a bow to dig several holes, but the water was slow to come. After praying to Benzaiten, water finally emerged. This event inspired the name "Osoroi" (遅の井, "slow well"), which references the area's historical name.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Highlights of Zenpukuji Park|url=http://www.tokyo-park.or.jp/park/format/view010.html |publisher=Tokyo Park Association |accessdate=2023-05-26}}</ref> In 1197 (Kenkyū 8), the shrine was established by enshrining the spirit of {{ill|Enoshima Shrine|lt=Enoshima Benzaiten|ja|江ノ島弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoshima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江島神社}}. This origin story is recounted in the ''Brief History of Zenpuku Benzaiten''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=116 Ichikishima Shrine|url=https://www.city.suginami.tokyo.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/hyouji/1007964.html|website=Suginami Ward Cultural Property Guide Signboard |publisher=Suginami Ward Board of Education|accessdate=2024-02-20|date=2016-01-18}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] era (1624–1644), the shrine was relocated from an island in a pond to its current location. Historically, during droughts, the shrine was a destination for rainmaking prayers from neighboring villages, such as those in present-day {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|qid=Q232655|lt=Nerima|1=Nerima}}, {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|qid=Q232635|lt=Itabashi|1=Itabashi}}, and {{ill|Nakano|en|Nakano|qid=Q346219|lt=Nakano|1=Nakano}} wards. 

A drum bridge that once led to the shrine was relocated to the grounds of Igusa Hachiman Shrine, preventing direct access to the main building today.<ref>{{Cite book |author=Ōtani Mitsuo |author2=Shigena Yoshiaki |date=1978-01 |chapter=Exploring Old Nishi-Ogikubo: The Story of Osoroi Ichikishima Shrine |title=Suginami Ward Historic Site Walk |series=Tokyo Historic Site Guide, Volume 15 |publisher=Gakuseisha |pages=51-52 |ncid=BN15561175}}</ref>

== Access ==

* Accessible by bus from {{ill|Nishi-Ogikubo Station|en|Nishi-Ogikubo Station|qid=Q801263|lt=Nishi-Ogikubo Station|1=Nishi-Ogikubo Station}} ({{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}), {{ill|Ogikubo Station|en|Ogikubo Station|qid=Q801294|lt=Ogikubo Station|1=Ogikubo Station}} (JR Chūō Line and {{ill|Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|en|Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|qid=Q1147028|lt=Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|1=Marunouchi Line}}), or {{ill|Kami-Shakujii Station|en|Kami-Shakujii Station|qid=Q842904|lt=Kami-Shakujii Station|1=Kami-Shakujii Station}} ({{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|qid=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line|1=Seibu Shinjuku Line}}).
* Admission is free, but the path to the main building is closed due to the lack of a bridge.

== References ==

{{Reflist|2}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book |author=Ōtani Mitsuo |author2=Shigena Yoshiaki |date=1978-01 |chapter=Exploring Old Nishi-Ogikubo: The Story of Osoroi Ichikishima Shrine |title=Suginami Ward Historic Site Walk |series=Tokyo Historic Site Guide, Volume 15 |publisher=Gakuseisha |pages=51-52 |ncid=BN15561175}}

== External Links ==

* [https://www.city.suginami.tokyo.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/hyouji/1007964.html 116 Ichikishima Shrine] - Suginami Ward Cultural Property Guide Signboard
* [http://www.tesshow.jp/suginami/shrine_zenpukuji_benten.html Osoroi Ichikishima Shrine] - Neko no Ashiato
